title: Orbis Intern
descriptionHtml:
Our internship program aims to support the development of high-potential individuals through an engaging professional learning experience at a performance and client-focused global investment management firm.

 

We provide undergraduate students an exciting opportunity to gain valuable knowledge and insight into several essential teams within Business Operations, Investment Research, Assurance, Legal and IT.

 

The internship is a 10-week competitively paid placement in our Bermuda office and is ideally completed between June and August.

Please visit our internship career site to learn more.

 

Duties and Responsibilities

Orbis’ internships are uniquely designed around your skills and interests to encourage personal development and growth. You will work alongside global teams and experts across the firm while receiving one-on-one mentorship to support your learning.

 

Specific responsibilities will vary depending on which team you work with, and as an intern you can expect to:

Gain exposure to a range of activities within an investment management firm
Work alongside  global teams to complete special projects and assist with standard tasks
Learn about the industry and the different investment strategies and funds that Orbis offers
 

Qualifications

About you

We are not looking for a specific background or a particular set of skills to fill a position - we are looking for self-starters who are prepared to push themselves to learn and grow. We value intellectual curiosity and an interest in the industry. Other attributes of people who excel in this internship include:

Demonstrated ability to think analytically, challenge the norms, and provide innovative solutions
Strong written and verbal communication skills
High level of accuracy and attention to detail
Thrives in both autonomous and collaborative environments
 

If you think these attributes describe you, this internship could be a great fit! Successful candidates will also meet the following:

Strong academic record
Ideally third or fourth year university/college student
Bermudian, spouse of a Bermudian, BOTC or PRC holder
 

Instructions for application

To complete your application, please submit your resume and transcripts (all post-secondary to this point; unofficial are accepted) before the deadline of 1 March 2026.
